ops.title: W4 model lane: much-larger Tinker arm + provider-agnostic scale-to-zero hosted serving eval + spike
ops.description: Per the approved 2026-07-18 E2E plan (user directive: local resident model stays lazy/light; double down on non-local). W4a: Tinker catalog recon → pinned hosted-only BASE_MODELS entry in athome (operator-idle check first; new entry only) → experiments/watcher-base-sweep-<arm>.toml → score-watcher run (≤$40 cap) vs 0.9371/0.9301. W4b: scale-to-zero provider survey (Modal, RunPod serverless, dedicated inference providers with custom-adapter hosting) at watcher duty cycle (~2/100 turns, bursty); daemon-side latency-budget measurement; ~$10 spike: promoted watcher artifact fused behind an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, eval-what-you-serve gate scored THROUGH that endpoint vs the 0.9291 local baseline. Serving lands as ONE abstraction in athome's serve layer — rapid-mlx local and hosted are backends of the same contract, not parallel routes. W4c: local lane maintenance-only; 9B fused-local spike deferred. Ends with a user serving decision recorded as a cc-notes note. Related: athome ledger 6815cf2 (9B deployment).

ops.text: provider matrix with per-criterion evidence journaled; latency budget measured and written down
big-arm score run lands .athome-metric.json under the $40 cap; AUC vs 0.9371/0.9301 journaled
spike: served-frame AUC through a hosted endpoint recorded vs 0.9291 local baseline; serving decision note exists
